user_message: Before my call with Northwind Traders, draft the follow-up. I met with them last week but can't remember the details.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sandra Taylor <sandra.taylor@northwindtra.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-26 09:23 EST
Subject: Follow-up: pipeline hygiene Review

There's been a change in our timeline for pipeline hygiene. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Sandra Taylor <sandra.taylor@northwindtra.com>
Date: 2024-02-09 12:39 EST
Subject: URGENT: pipeline hygiene Issue at Northwind Traders

Our team encountered some challenges with pipeline hygiene. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Sandra Taylor <sandra.taylor@northwindtra.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-27 16:22 EST
Subject: pipeline hygiene - Action Required

Just checking in on the pipeline hygiene proposal. Our Head of Digital is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: pipeline hygiene Discussion - Northwind Traders
Date: 2024-01-07
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft)

[00:01:30] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into pipeline hygiene.

[00:08:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:11:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:13:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for timeline constraints is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:18:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): Let me add some context -- we've been working on vendor dependencies and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:20:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): Let me add some context -- we've been working on partner engagement and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:25:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:28:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): Let me add some context -- we've been working on security compliance and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-M trap (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-M failure mode (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct pipeline hygiene decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-M -- Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
